Alex has always been a conservative reader, preferring stories that align with traditional values. Recently, Alex started exploring novels that challenge their views on love and relationships. These books introduced new perspectives that were quite different from what Alex was used to. Each novel was carefully chosen to provide a deeper understanding of diverse experiences. The first novel took Alex 4 days to finish, which was a significant amount of time. It contained 120 pages, which is a moderate length for a novel. The second novel was longer, with 180 pages, and it took Alex 6 days to complete. This novel required more time due to its increased length. The third novel was the longest, with 240 pages, and it took Alex 8 days to finish. This novel was the most challenging in terms of both length and content.  

Alex read the same number of pages each day for each novel, which helped maintain a consistent reading pace. This consistency made it easier for Alex to manage their time. Calculating the average pages per day across all three novels required some simple math. To find the average, Alex needed to sum the total pages and divide by the total number of days. The first novel had 120 pages and took 4 days, so the daily rate was 30 pages. The second novel had 180 pages and took 6 days, so the daily rate was also 30 pages. The third novel had 240 pages and took 8 days, so the daily rate was 30 pages. This consistency across all three novels was impressive.  

The same number of pages per day meant that Alex could maintain a steady reading schedule. This approach helped Alex avoid burnout and stay engaged with the material. Each novel provided a different perspective on love and relationships, which was both enlightening and thought-provoking. The first novel introduced a more traditional view of love, while the second novel offered a more modern take. The third novel explored the complexities of love in a more nuanced way. These stories encouraged Alex to think beyond their own experiences.  

Reading the same number of pages each day also helped Alex track their progress. This tracking was essential for maintaining motivation and staying on schedule.
